_comment: Agent-authored GIIGNL §3.2.1 narrative-prose findings that correct the operating STATUS of rows in GIIGNL's operating-only liq/regas TABLES. The narrative sometimes states a train listed (untagged) in the operating table is not actually operating; report_diff moves the named unit(s) out of the operating total into nonop_rows, carrying the citation. Capacity NUMBERS are never changed here (Reconciliation SOP §5.6 prefers the tabular value), and nothing is auto-applied to GEM (§3.8) — this only makes the GIIGNL side of the diff internally consistent with GIIGNL's own prose. Authored per-edition; re-read the narrative pages (2026: liquefaction pp.28-31, regasification pp.48-52) each batch. `unit` is matched as a token of the report row's site_name+trains (e.g. 'F' matches 'Bontang Train F').

operating_status_corrections.source: GIIGNL 2026 Annual Report, liquefaction narrative p.31: "At Bontang LNG, Train E remains on standby, with only Trains G and H currently in operation due to limited feedgas availability." Train E is already tagged (Mothballed) in the table; Train F is listed untagged but the prose confirms only G and H operate.

_nonop_comment: Narrative corroborations of a GEM NON-operating unit that has NO GIIGNL operating-table row (the unit ceased/retired, so GIIGNL drops it from the operating table, but the prose names it). report_diff fills the unit's giignl_narrative_mention and clears its 'GEM has, GIIGNL doesn't' flag. Keyed by GEM terminal name (matched against the GEM project key) + GEM unit name.

nonop_corroborations.source: GIIGNL 2026 Annual Report narrative p.28: "the NWS Train 2 terminal in Australia, with a capacity of 3.4 MTPA, has permanently ceased operations" (also p.31: "Woodside has definitively ceased operations at Train 2"). GIIGNL drops Train 2 from the operating table accordingly; GEM has it retired — they agree.
